About Me

I go further
than the surface.

That's the one thing I won't compromise on — how well I understand the brand or audience I'm writing for.

I read a lot. Not randomly — intentionally. Right now I'm working through books on consumer psychology, brand positioning, and business strategy, and I apply what I learn immediately. That's just how I'm wired.

I notice patterns — what works across industries, what hooks land, what messaging structures convert and which ones just exist. I'm genuinely interested in why things work, not just what works.

And I have a personal rule: if another brand can pick up the same copy and use it, it isn't good enough. Everything I write has to be specific enough to only belong to the brand I'm writing for.

What I've

Brand Audit & Content Strategy · Spec Work

Mitchell Elegbe,
CEO of Interswitch

I studied 7 executives across Nigerian fintech and global payments — not because anyone asked me to, but because I spotted a gap. Their LinkedIn presence is almost entirely reactive. Events. Awards. Reposts. Nobody was building an original voice. So I built the strategy I'd use to change that: 8 content formats, a competitor analysis, and a full content plan for a founder with 24 years of building behind him.

My Copywriting System

How I think
before I write.

"Copy that another brand can pick up and use is not good enough. Every line has to earn its place."

Before I write anything, I define the A → B: where is this person right now, and where do they need to be after reading this? Every decision flows from that.

Then I write rough. Multiple versions. I don't trust the first idea. I test each line: can I picture it? Can a competitor say it? Would a real person say this out loud? If it fails those — it goes.

What survives gets refined — made specific, made concrete, given rhythm. What doesn't, gets deleted. That's the whole system.

01
Define A → B

Where is the reader now? Where must they be after reading? Every word serves that movement.

02
Get Raw Clarity

Who is this for? What does it compete against? What do you believe that others don't?

03
Write Rough

Dump ideas fast. Write 3–5 hooks minimum. The first idea is rarely the strongest.

04
Filter Hard

Rubbish Test. Generic Detector. "Only We Can Say This" test. Fails any of them — gone.

05
Make It Concrete

Keep asking "what does this actually look like?" until it's specific enough to picture.

06
Facts Over Opinions

"High quality" is an opinion. "Plans your week in 10 seconds every Sunday" is a fact.

07
Add Clarity Tools

Contrast, analogy, conflict, parallelism — tools that make copy land harder and stay longer.

08
Edit Like a Killer

Remove a sentence. If nothing breaks — delete it. Every line earns its place or it's gone.

The Rubbish Test

  • Can I visualize it?
  • Can it be proven true or false?
  • Can a competitor say the exact same thing?
  • Weak on any of these — delete it

The Generic Detector

  • Google the phrase in quotes
  • Overused? Remove it
  • Sounds like marketing speak? Remove it
  • Would a real person say this face-to-face?

The "Only We" Test

  • Remove your brand name from the line
  • Can another brand use it? Trash it
  • Can only you say it? Keep it
  • This is where real differentiation lives
Clear
Easy to understand on first read
Concrete
Easy to picture. Specific, not abstract
Specific
Hard to copy. Uniquely theirs
Provable
Easy to believe. Built on facts, not claims
